{{Quote|Hoping his son would follow him into the Imperial military, Boes Tark trained his son in survival skills, as well as personal and fire combat.|General Airen Cracken, "Wanted by Cracken"|<ref name="WBC" />}}
'''Boes Tark''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="GG10" /> who was [[Marriage/Legends|married]] to his wife, [[Lanaxa Tark|Lanaxa]].<ref name="WBC" /> In [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]],<ref name="WBCdate">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], which notes [[Moxin Tark]]'s age as thirty, is set three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which the ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. ''Wanted by Cracken'' must therefore be set in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], and Moxin's [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] year must be [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]].</ref> while on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bandonia]], they [[Reproduction/Legends|had]] a [[Youngling/Legends|child]]. Naming the young boy [[Moxin Tark|Moxin]],<ref name="WBC" /> they raised him in a life of luxury and privilege on the [[Tark family estate]]. Boes would serve as a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and desired for Moxin to follow in his [[Limb/Legends|footsteps]] in Imperial military service, bringing fame to his family.<ref name="GG10" /> In preparation for this, Boes trained his son in combat and survival, but Moxin was disillusioned with his life and longed for [[Realspace/Legends|space]] adventure.<ref name="WBC" />

Despite his father's efforts, Moxin would grow to hate both his father and his father's military ways and became a runaway at age eighteen. He eventually turned to [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]] and,<ref name="GG10" /> by [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="WBCdate" /> was wanted by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], featuring in [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken's]] [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] ''[[Wanted by Cracken]]'', which included a brief mention of Boes.<ref name="WBC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Boes Tark was first mention in the [[1993]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]'s [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]], [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]].<ref name="WBC" />
